original_query: SELECT EMP.SAL + BONUS.COMM, SUM(BONUS.SAL + BONUS.SAL + 100) OVER (PARTITION BY BONUS.JOB RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) FROM EMP INNER JOIN BONUS ON EMP.ENAME = BONUS.ENAME AND EMP.DEPTNO = 10
rewritten_query:
WITH base AS (
    SELECT 
        e.SAL AS emp_sal, 
        b.COMM, 
        b.SAL AS bonus_sal, 
        b.JOB
    FROM EMP e
    INNER JOIN BONUS b ON e.ENAME = b.ENAME
    WHERE e.DEPTNO = 10
),
agg AS (
    SELECT 
        JOB, 
        SUM(2 * bonus_sal + 100) AS total
    FROM base
    GROUP BY JOB
)
SELECT 
    base.emp_sal + base.COMM,
    agg.total
FROM base
JOIN agg ON base.JOB = agg.JOB

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace window function with GROUP BY and JOIN to avoid expensive sort operation
Simplify BONUS.SAL + BONUS.SAL + 100 to 2 * BONUS.SAL + 100

FROM DEPT
WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT 2 * DEPTNO FROM DEPT)

FROM (VALUES ('table'), ('view'), ('foreign table')) AS t(X)
WHERE UPPER(X) = 'TABLE'

   FROM EMP

)
SELECT t6.JOB, t8.NAME
FROM t6

) t8 ON t6.JOB = t8.NAME AND t6.f9 = t8.f2
GROUP BY t6.JOB, t8.NAME

)

WITH filtered_emp AS (
    SELECT JOB, SUM(SAL) AS EXPR1 
    FROM EMP 
    WHERE ENAME = 'A' 
    GROUP BY JOB
)
SELECT 
    fe.JOB, 
    CAST(fe.EXPR1 * t7.f1 AS INTEGER) 
FROM filtered_emp fe
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT NAME, COUNT(*) AS f1 
    FROM DEPT 
    WHERE NAME IN (SELECT JOB FROM filtered_emp)
    GROUP BY NAME
) AS t7 ON fe.JOB = t7.NAME;

original_query: SELECT t6.JOB FROM (SELECT JOB, DEPTNO + EMPNO AS f9 FROM EMP WHERE ENAME = 'A' GROUP BY JOB, DEPTNO + EMPNO) AS t6 LEFT JOIN (SELECT NAME, DEPTNO + 5 AS f2 FROM DEPT GROUP BY NAME, DEPTNO + 5) AS t8 ON t6.JOB = t8.NAME AND t6.f9 = t8.f2 GROUP BY t6.JOB

original_query: SELECT t12.SAL FROM (SELECT * FROM (SELECT EMPNO, ENAME, JOB, MGR, HIREDATE, SAL, COMM, SLACKER, SAL AS SAL0, SAL = 4 AS f9 FROM EMPNULLABLES WHERE DEPTNO = 20 AND SAL > 1000) AS t11 WHERE SAL > 1000) AS t12 INNER JOIN (SELECT t14.SAL, t17.SAL AS SAL0, t17.f9 FROM (SELECT EMPNO, ENAME, JOB, MGR, HIREDATE, SAL, COMM, SLACKER FROM EMPNULLABLES WHERE DEPTNO = 20 AND SAL > 1000) AS t14 INNER JOIN (SELECT SAL, SAL = 4 AS f9 FROM EMPNULLABLES WHERE DEPTNO = 20 AND SAL > 1000 GROUP BY SAL, SAL = 4) AS t17 ON t14.SAL = t17.SAL OR t17.f9 GROUP BY t14.SAL, t17.SAL, t17.f9 HAVING t17.SAL = t14.SAL) AS t20 ON t12.SAL = t20.SAL0 AND t12.f9 = t20.f9

original_query: SELECT * FROM (SELECT DEPTNO FROM EMP WHERE DEPTNO < 4) AS t7 INNER JOIN (SELECT * FROM (SELECT DEPTNO FROM EMP WHERE DEPTNO > 7 UNION ALL SELECT DEPTNO FROM EMP) AS t11 WHERE DEPTNO < 4) AS t12 ON t7.DEPTNO = t12.DEPTNO
rewritten_query:
WITH dept_less_4 AS (
    SELECT DEPTNO FROM EMP WHERE DEPTNO < 4
)
SELECT t7.DEPTNO, t12.DEPTNO
FROM dept_less_4 t7
JOIN dept_less_4 t12 ON t7.DEPTNO = t12.DEPTNO

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Remove the redundant branch in the UNION ALL where DEPTNO > 7 is later filtered by DEPTNO < 4, which always yields zero rows
Use a CTE to avoid scanning the same table twice with identical filters

original_query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(EXPR0), 0), T2 FROM (SELECT t5.T2, COUNT(CASE WHEN EMP1.DEPTNO = 0 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS EXPR0 FROM EMP AS EMP1, (VALUES (1)) AS t5 (T2) GROUP BY t5.T2 UNION ALL SELECT t8.T2, COUNT(CASE WHEN EMP2.DEPTNO = 0 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS EXPR0 FROM EMP AS EMP2, (VALUES (2)) AS t8 (T2) GROUP BY t8.T2) AS t11 GROUP BY T2
rewritten_query:
WITH counts AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt 
    FROM EMP 
    WHERE DEPTNO = 0
)
SELECT counts.cnt, t.T2
FROM (VALUES (1), (2)) AS t(T2)
CROSS JOIN counts;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Avoid duplicate full table scans by computing the count once and then generating the two rows with a cross join
Replace COUNT(CASE WHEN EMP.DEPTNO = 0 THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) with simpler COUNT(*) WHERE DEPTNO = 0
Eliminate redundant outer GROUP BY since each T2 value already has exactly one row

original_query: SELECT t4.NAME FROM (SELECT NAME, DEPTNO, DEPTNO - 10 AS DEPTNOMINUS FROM DEPT) AS t4 INNER JOIN (SELECT DEPTNO, SAL + 1 AS f9 FROM EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO, SAL + 1) AS t6 ON t4.DEPTNOMINUS = t6.f9 AND t4.DEPTNO = t6.DEPTNO

FROM DEPT d

    FROM EMP
    WHERE SAL = DEPTNO - 11
) e ON d.DEPTNO = e.DEPTNO

WITH deptno_set AS (
    SELECT deptno
    FROM emp
    WHERE empno < 20
)

       e.deptno IN (SELECT deptno FROM deptno_set) AS d
FROM emp AS e;
